title: Capacity Building for qPCR-Based Halal Authentication Laboratories
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   food     authentication     is     essential     for     protecting     consumer     trust  ,   supporting     religious     compliance  ,   and     promoting     international     trade  .   Quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   is     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     highly     specific     method     to     verify     animal     species   in   food     products  ,   ensuring     compliance     with   halal   standards  .   Despite     its     potential  ,   many     laboratories     lack     adequate     infrastructure  ,   trained     personnel  ,   and     standardized     protocols     for     reliable   halal testing.   Strengthening     laboratory     capacity     for     qPCR-based   halal   authentication     will     improve     detection     of   non-halal   adulteration  ,   reduce     food     fraud  ,   and     enhance     certification     systems  . Investment in   infrastructure  ,   training     programs  ,   and     quality     management     systems     is     critical     to     ensure     consistent  ,   reproducible  ,   and     internationally     recognized     results  .   Such     efforts     will     reinforce   halal   assurance     systems  ,   boost     consumer     confidence  ,   and     expand     market     access     for   halal-  certified     products     globally  .
